short_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, Bowled!! All over! West Indies have been bowled out for a mere 55. Rampaul could not resist long
full_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, Bowled!! All over! West Indies have been bowled out for a mere 55. Rampaul could not resist longer. He saw a bit of flight and went for the slog sweep, missed it compeletely and the ball crashes into the stumps.

short_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, no run, googly outside off, Rampaul read it late and let it pass
full_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, no run, googly outside off, Rampaul read it late and let it pass

short_text: Tymal Mills to Rampaul, 1 run, back of a length on off, Rampaul drops it into the off-side and crosses over. Mills is do
full_text: Tymal Mills to Rampaul, 1 run, back of a length on off, Rampaul drops it into the off-side and crosses over. Mills is done with the ball and it is an impressive return to T20Is,

full_text: Tymal Mills to Rampaul, no run, fullish delivery around off, Rampaul sits back in the crease and plays inside the line, beaten on the outside edge

full_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, 1 run, dipping slower ball and Hosein works it to the left of mid-on, for a single

full_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, no run, 141kph short of length delivery outside off, Hosein is beaten by the extra bit of pace and is defeated on the outside edge. He was trying to steer it to third man

full_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, 2 runs, short and width on offer, Hosein cracks it in front of square and Bairstow tidies up in the deep, just two

full_text: Tymal Mills to Rampaul, 1 run, 137kph good length ball around off, Rampaul angles it down to third man

full_text: Adil Rashid to Akeal Hosein, no run, googly around off, Hosein hangs back, reads it from the surface and defends it down the wicket

full_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, 1 run, turning into Rampaul and he nurdles it backward of square leg, for a single

full_text: Adil Rashid to Rampaul, no run, drifting across the left hander, Rampaul prods forward to defend and plays for the turn, beaten on the outside edge

short_text: Adil Rashid to Obed McCoy, Caught by Roy!! This is just plain atrocious batting. Even calling it reckless will be puttin
full_text: Adil Rashid to Obed McCoy, Caught by Roy!! This is just plain atrocious batting. Even calling it reckless will be putting it mildly. They are 8 down, the skipper has just walked back and McCoy decides he still has it in him to clear the field. No, he doesn't. Rashid is a smart operator, dragged the length back a bit and induced the mistimed loft, simple catch at long-on. Rashid is

short_text: Adil Rashid to Pollard, Caught by Bairstow!! There goes West Indies' last hope. Pollard had to bat through and get them
full_text: Adil Rashid to Pollard, Caught by Bairstow!! There goes West Indies' last hope. Pollard had to bat through and get them past 120 at least but he too departs trying to clear the rope. Rashid didn't get perturbed by Pollard's leg movement before he delivered the ball, just tossed it up and invited the big hit, the West Indies skipper took the bait and went for the loft, miscued to long-on and Bairstow takes it calmly.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, no run, stifled appeal for LBW. There was two noises. Nips back in from a length and catche
full_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, no run, stifled appeal for LBW. There was two noises. Nips back in from a length and catches Hosein on the move, there must have been an inside edge onto the pad and hence the umpire was not interested

short_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, no run, off-pace delivery and Hosein wanted to run it down to third man, he's through the s
full_text: Tymal Mills to Akeal Hosein, no run, off-pace delivery and Hosein wanted to run it down to third man, he's through the shot early and the under-edge rolls back to Buttler

full_text: Tymal Mills to Pollard, 1 run, fullish delivery on the pads, Pollard flicks straight to deep square leg

full_text: Tymal Mills to Pollard, 1 run, back of the hand slower ball, Pollard plays it with soft hands in front of point and crosses over

short_text: Adil Rashid to Russell, Bowled!! Oh my! Apart from Moeen, every England bowler has picked a wicket in his first over. Ra
full_text: Adil Rashid to Russell, Bowled!! Oh my! Apart from Moeen, every England bowler has picked a wicket in his first over. Rashid comes on and cleans up Russell. This is just a straight ball, released it from front of the hand, Russell prods forward and plays for the turn, leaving a gap between bat and pad for the ball to sneak through, crashes into middle stump. Getting worse and worse for West Indies.

short_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, okay, I'll give you the respect here kind of leave to this short ball from Pollard
full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, okay, I'll give you the respect here kind of leave to this short ball from Pollard

full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, stays leg-side and defends this short of length delivery towards point

short_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, that is the third time in three balls that Pollard has not middled Jordan's incoming angle. T
full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, that is the third time in three balls that Pollard has not middled Jordan's incoming angle. Troubled by a good length ball, full ball, now by back of a length too. Catches the thigh pad this time

short_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, again inside edge onto pad, half shout for LBW. Jordan rushing Pollard with his incoming angl
full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, again inside edge onto pad, half shout for LBW. Jordan rushing Pollard with his incoming angle, big inside edge and it was bat hitting pad too

short_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, Pollard beaten on the flick, well he got an inside edge onto pad to save his wicket. Else thi
full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, Pollard beaten on the flick, well he got an inside edge onto pad to save his wicket. Else this length ball would have sent him back LBW

full_text: Jordan to Pollard, 2 run, short of length delivery on the leg stump, Pollard glances it to backward square leg

short_text: Tymal Mills to Russell, no run, who cares if it's 42/6 or 142/6, I will come out and swing hard says Dre Russ. This coul
full_text: Tymal Mills to Russell, no run, who cares if it's 42/6 or 142/6, I will come out and swing hard says Dre Russ. This could be very interesting if he can counterattack. Full ball angling across, he missed this though

short_text: Tymal Mills to Pooran, Caught by Buttler!! Buttler's not even celebrating. It's been that easy for England. Pooran's hor
full_text: Tymal Mills to Pooran, Caught by Buttler!! Buttler's not even celebrating. It's been that easy for England. Pooran's horrible run of form of late - which is very hard to believe with the talent he has - continues. Just a gentle full, drive me through cover ball from Tymal Mills, Pooran does try but only nicks off. Buttler gently throws the ball in the air. It's a very sad looking 42/6 on the board for West Indies.

full_text: Tymal Mills to Pooran, , slower delivery down leg, third wide of the over

full_text: Tymal Mills to Pooran, no run, short and wide outside off stump, Pooran thinks of the pull, realises it's too risky to fetch it from that far, lets it go

full_text: Tymal Mills to Pooran, no run, . Good length delivery outside off stump, Pooran slashes looking to play off-side, but makes no contact

full_text: Jordan to Pollard, no run, oh where's that gone kind of look from Pollard. He played the pull shot to this short delivery, but this one went off the bottom of the bat to the off-side

short_text: Jordan to Dwayne Bravo, Caught by Bairstow!! Oh bit of a shocking dismissal in the sense that Bravo played a really attr
full_text: Jordan to Dwayne Bravo, Caught by Bairstow!! Oh bit of a shocking dismissal in the sense that Bravo played a really attractive cut shot, but Bairstow takes a low grab at point. Nobody thought it was going to fetch a wicket. Good length and outside off stump, Bravo wanted another hit past point, but he picked out the fielder. Can't believe his luck. Lovely technique from Bairstow to stay low. Of course it helps if it comes straight at you.

short_text: Jordan to Dwayne Bravo, , what a shot from Bravo. Short and wide outside off stump, Bravo backs away but also bends a bi
full_text: Jordan to Dwayne Bravo, , what a shot from Bravo. Short and wide outside off stump, Bravo backs away but also bends a bit to cut that nicely over point and beat the fielder in the deep

full_text: Moeen Ali to Pooran, no run, blocked to short mid-wicket

full_text: Moeen Ali to Dwayne Bravo, 1 run, turning back in, wristed towards square leg

short_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, Caught by Malan!! In the air again, taken again. Gayle is livid as he walks back. He probably want
full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, Caught by Malan!! In the air again, taken again. Gayle is livid as he walks back. He probably wanted make full use of the last powerplay ball to get some runs, but what's in a ball and why care of field restrictions when you are the Universe Boss clearing the biggest of boundaries in the world. This short ball interests him, wasn't really short, so he thought he could nail the pull shot, but top-edged this high over mid-wicket. Fielder moves back and takes a good catch. Bowlers have got Gayle with the bumper before, Mills gets him now.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, , anything that's slightly short will interest Gayle because he doesn't have to move much. The han
full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, , anything that's slightly short will interest Gayle because he doesn't have to move much. The hands take over in slashing this slower delivery over point. Not middled, but who cares

short_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, ah no! Not a good sign to see Gayle just have a wild swing with feet fully glued on the cr
full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, ah no! Not a good sign to see Gayle just have a wild swing with feet fully glued on the crease. Yes bit of a slot ball, but Gayle was in no position to meet the ball because he never moved that front foot. Lucky he didn't edge this

short_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, lovely slower ball yorker, dug out to short fine leg. Where are you going DJ? It's Gayle a
full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, lovely slower ball yorker, dug out to short fine leg. Where are you going DJ? It's Gayle at the crease, so a quick single is very tough

short_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, . Short of a length delivery just outside off stump, Gayle looks to slash and misses becau
full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, . Short of a length delivery just outside off stump, Gayle looks to slash and misses because of extra bounce

full_text: Tymal Mills to Gayle, no run, short of length delivery outside off stump, Gayle punches it to the left of extra-cover

full_text: Moeen Ali to Dwayne Bravo, no run, leans forward and meets this very well

full_text: Moeen Ali to Dwayne Bravo, no run, flighted delivery on the stumps, Bravo keeps it out

short_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, Caught by Morgan!! Mistimed, mistimed, taken at mid-on. Hetmyer shakes his head. He knows he's thr
full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, Caught by Morgan!! Mistimed, mistimed, taken at mid-on. Hetmyer shakes his head. He knows he's thrown it away. Got two boundaries, he's in form too, so he thought he could put Mo away, but Mo does Mo things - react well to what the batter does. Hetmyer was on the back foot to pull this, but this actually wasn't really short for the shot. Hetmyer was caught on the crease a bit, but he went through without a proper base and mishit for an easy grab at mid-on. Two wickets for Mo.

short_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, closer to off-stump and Hetmyer pushes it to extra cover
full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, closer to off-stump and Hetmyer pushes it to extra cover

short_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, , this is a brilliant inside-out hit from Hetmyer. Again the use of the feet and he left very late
full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, , this is a brilliant inside-out hit from Hetmyer. Again the use of the feet and he left very late, again the bat coming down straight and not across the line, meets this well and gets it over wide mid-off

short_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, , good hit from Hetty! Unlike Simmons, he stepped out and brought out the straight batted loft, ba
full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, , good hit from Hetty! Unlike Simmons, he stepped out and brought out the straight batted loft, backed himself to clear mid-on and he did so quite comfortably

full_text: Woakes to Hetmyer, 1 run, fullish delivery outside off stump, Hetmyer drives it to deep cover

full_text: Woakes to Gayle, 1 run, short of length delivery, Gayle guides it to point

full_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, blocked easily this time

short_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, uppish push, doesn't carry to short cover. Good length and on the off stump, Gayle checks the d
full_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, uppish push, doesn't carry to short cover. Good length and on the off stump, Gayle checks the defence, luckily doesn't carry

short_text: Woakes to Gayle, , in the airrr, in the gap. That was almost going to hand at backward point. Gayle making a statement.
full_text: Woakes to Gayle, , in the airrr, in the gap. That was almost going to hand at backward point. Gayle making a statement. Short of a length with a bit of width this time, Gayle cuts really hard, enough power to beat the left-side dive at backward point

short_text: Woakes to Gayle, , punched off the back foot, oh mid-off dives and misses the ball. Woakes wanted a better effort having
full_text: Woakes to Gayle, , punched off the back foot, oh mid-off dives and misses the ball. Woakes wanted a better effort having bowled a pretty decent short of length delivery. Gayle hit this to the right of mid-off and it escaped for four. Gayle off the mark

short_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, half shout for caught behind, not given. Mo says brushed the thigh pad and it's a wicket-m
full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, half shout for caught behind, not given. Mo says brushed the thigh pad and it's a wicket-maiden for this very smart cricketer. Was indeed a leg-side drifter but Hetmyer got into a tangle and missed the glance

full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, covers the line and keeps it calm

full_text: Moeen Ali to Hetmyer, no run, flighted delivery on the stumps, Hetmyer defends

short_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, Caught by Livingstone!! "Brainless batting," says Nass Hussain on air. This is atrocious cricket f
full_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, Caught by Livingstone!! "Brainless batting," says Nass Hussain on air. This is atrocious cricket from Simmons. Morgan had a man at deep mid-wicket, and Simmons steps out to slog this straight to the fielder who didn't hit to move an inch. Who said offies can't succeed against right-handers as thought to be these days? Look at Mo. He keeps things very simple, no slingly or funky stuff. Just makes sure the batter doesn't get under the ball by watching the batter's feet till the last moment. Simmons ran at him,. so Mo dragged his length back and that meant Simmons got this on the bottom of the bat. Keep it simple stuff from Moeen, the smart operator.

short_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, no run, flighted delivery on the off stump, Simmons defends
full_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, no run, flighted delivery on the off stump, Simmons defends

short_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, short of length delivery outside off stump, Gayle lets it pass
full_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, short of length delivery outside off stump, Gayle lets it pass

short_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, very watchful defence in front of the pad to this short of length delivery, played with an angl
full_text: Woakes to Gayle, no run, very watchful defence in front of the pad to this short of length delivery, played with an angled bat to backward point

short_text: Woakes to Simmons, 1 run, short of length delivery, Simmons knocks it to deep square leg
full_text: Woakes to Simmons, 1 run, short of length delivery, Simmons knocks it to deep square leg

short_text: Woakes to Lewis, Caught by Moeen Ali!! In the airrrr, only height on this one, but it's clearing the infield. Moeen Ali
full_text: Woakes to Lewis, Caught by Moeen Ali!! In the airrrr, only height on this one, but it's clearing the infield. Moeen Ali runs back from mid-off, never easy these ones, but he does so well to keep cool and keep his eyes on the ball looking over his right shoulder and pouching it with both hands facing the boundary. Big wicket for England. Lovely piece of slower delivery in the slot from Woakes, Lewis thought he could thump it down the ground, but it took the bottom of the bat. Brilliantly judged grab from Mo.

short_text: Woakes to Lewis, no run, shortish and just outside off stump, Lewis defends off the back foot
full_text: Woakes to Lewis, no run, shortish and just outside off stump, Lewis defends off the back foot

full_text: Woakes to Simmons, 1 run, short of length delivery on the off stump, Simmons walks across and tucks behind square

short_text: Moeen Ali to Lewis, , and there you have the Evin Lewis loft to an overpitched ball. In the slot to tee off, Moeen Ali f
full_text: Moeen Ali to Lewis, , and there you have the Evin Lewis loft to an overpitched ball. In the slot to tee off, Moeen Ali for some reason overpitched after bowling tight, Lewis took the invitation and took the safest route possible in cricket - back over the bowler's head. Pressure-releasing last ball for West Indies

short_text: Moeen Ali to Lewis, no run, oh short ball but the plus with Moeen Ali is he didn't give the width, Lewis goes back and p
full_text: Moeen Ali to Lewis, no run, oh short ball but the plus with Moeen Ali is he didn't give the width, Lewis goes back and punches hard, finds the fielder at cover. Very lucky for the bowler

full_text: Moeen Ali to Lewis, no run, flighted delivery just outside off stump, Lewis defends off the front foot

full_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, 1 run, flatter and just outside off stump, easy nudge to the mid-wicket gap and West Indies are off the mark

full_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, no run, shortish and outside off stump, Simmons goes back and punches it towards short cover

full_text: Moeen Ali to Simmons, no run, floated full and outside off stump, Simmons drives it back to the bowler

short_text: Pollard to Buttler, , that'll do for England, - Pollard telegraphed the slower ball and Buttler was waiting on it, moves
full_text: Pollard to Buttler, , that'll do for England, - Pollard telegraphed the slower ball and Buttler was waiting on it, moves across and then swats it past square leg, beats fine leg to his left and that's that for this game

short_text: Pollard to Buttler, 2 runs, back of a length and well wide of off, Buttler waits on it and cuts it backwarde of square o
full_text: Pollard to Buttler, 2 runs, back of a length and well wide of off, Buttler waits on it and cuts it backwarde of square on the off-side - McCoy runs across to his left from deep point, puts in the dive and prevents the boundary

short_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, no run, banged in short and angling down leg, Morgan swivelled across on the pull and missed - an
full_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, no run, banged in short and angling down leg, Morgan swivelled across on the pull and missed - an excited appeal for a , but West Indies aren't reviewing

short_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, no run, the slower ball now - Morgan spots it late and then defends it solidly
full_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, no run, the slower ball now - Morgan spots it late and then defends it solidly

short_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, , almost another stunner! Hayden Walsh there and he was well inside the ropes - did all the hard y
full_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, , almost another stunner! Hayden Walsh there and he was well inside the ropes - did all the hard yards, moved across from deep square leg - threw himself one-handed (as Akeal did), but can only get a fingertip and palmed it to the boundary ropes - banged in short and Morgan swivelled across on the pull over square leg

short_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, 1 run, the slower yorker now, dipped in front of the right-hander, Buttler watched it all along a
full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, 1 run, the slower yorker now, dipped in front of the right-hander, Buttler watched it all along and pushed it towards mid-off for a single

full_text: Obed McCoy to Morgan, 1 run, banged in short and rising onto the left-hander, Morgan rides the bounce and drops it in front of mid-on for a quick single

full_text: Akeal Hosein to Morgan, no run, the slider to end an excellent night with the ball for Akeal - went straight on and nearly caught Morgan in the crease, gets his bat down in time and squirts it to the on-side

short_text: Akeal Hosein to Livingstone, Caught&Bowled!! What a grab from Akeal, no wonder he's running away in joy - they might los
full_text: Akeal Hosein to Livingstone, Caught&Bowled!! What a grab from Akeal, no wonder he's running away in joy - they might lose this, but they're giving England a hard time. The umpires are checking for something, is it for a fair catch? The soft signal is OUT - we'll have a look, but this is sensational from Akeal, if it's a clean catch. He went one-handed as he threw himself to his left, grabbed the ball and then ensured that it didn't hit the turf as he completed the catch - that's clean for me and it's clean enough for the third umpire as well - Yes, fingers underneath the ball, we have had some stormers already in this tournament, but this catch will take some beating. He did see plenty of angles, but with the soft signal considered, there was no way he was going to deny Akeal his moment of glory. Had to ensure that he was in complete control and he surely was, plenty of high fives from his mates, and it's a deserving one. Very full and on the pads, the drift was what did Livingstone in, was early into the flick, the ball caught the leading edge and lobbed to the left of the bowler. On another day - Livingstone might just have survived.

full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, 1 run, another slower ball from McCoy - Buttler backs away and muscles it through mid-wicket for a single - Gayle tumbled but can't make the stop

short_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, , another slower ball and Buttler is done in by the lack of pace - was looking to play the revers
full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, , another slower ball and Buttler is done in by the lack of pace - was looking to play the reverse paddle and missed, bounced through to the keeper - but McCoy has overstepped and

short_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, no run, short of a length and at the stumps, turned to the on-side
full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, no run, short of a length and at the stumps, turned to the on-side

short_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, Moeen Ali Run Out!! Has Moeen Ali run himself out? He was a fair way down the pitch when sent bac
full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, Moeen Ali Run Out!! Has Moeen Ali run himself out? He was a fair way down the pitch when sent back - an awkward throw, but McCoy collected it cleanly and broke the stumps. Reckon Moeen Ali is short - yes, he's well short, by a couple of yards. A third wicket for West Indies, they're at least giving themselves something to take back from the game - horrendous running from Moeen Ali though, ran blind and never looked at his partner, sent back late and he was never beating the throw, if it was a decent one. Was another slower cutter that reared onto Buttler - startled him as he nudged it towards short third man, was busy ball-watching and didn't look at his partner, even as he took a step down the wicket, enough for Moeen Ali to respond. The return was awkward, but McCoy lunged across and then broke the stumps.

full_text: Obed McCoy to Buttler, 2 runs, slower short ball wide of off, stabbed away backward of square on the off-side and Buttler hurries back for the second

full_text: Akeal Hosein to Moeen Ali, no run, Akeal drifts one on the pads, Moeen was looking to tuck it to the on-side and missed, off the pad and rolled behind

short_text: Akeal Hosein to Bairstow, Caught&Bowled!! Smart catch from Akeal - some more joy on the faces of the West Indians. Dragg
full_text: Akeal Hosein to Bairstow, Caught&Bowled!! Smart catch from Akeal - some more joy on the faces of the West Indians. Dragged his length back by a fraction and made Bairstow reach out on the drive, he did so with hard hands, couldn't control the shot - off the inner half of the bat and a simple return catch to the bowler. Had impressed in the CPL, got called in as a replacement and now has a first Word Cup wicket. The replays shows that was the drifter pushed with the index fingers. Lovely shape back into the right-hander and that foxed Bairstow, who walks off shaking the head.

full_text: Rampaul to Bairstow, , back-to-back boundaries for Bairstow - width on offer and Bairstow threw his hands on the drive, through the gap at cover-point and there's no need to run for those

full_text: Rampaul to Bairstow, , , tad too full from Rampaul, Bairstow leans forward and then drives it straight of mid-off, he gave up early and the ball runs away to the ropes

short_text: Rampaul to Roy, Caught by Gayle!! Gentle catch and West Indies have something to cheer for in the field - think was the
full_text: Rampaul to Roy, Caught by Gayle!! Gentle catch and West Indies have something to cheer for in the field - think was the slower ball and Roy was completely undone by the change in variation - was through with the clip even before the ball arrived, jabbed with hard hands and hit it in the air, and into the grateful hands of Gayle at short mid-wicket. Can they sneak in a few more wickets?
